problem.title: Find Peak Element
problem.description: A peak element is an element that is strictly greater than its neighbors.
assumptions: O(log n) solution exists using binary search.

example.explanation: nums[2]=3 is a peak since nums[1]=2 < 3 > nums[3]=1. Return any peak index.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Binary Search [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Binary search works here because if nums[mid] < nums[mid+1], there must be a peak to the right (the sequence is still ascending). Otherwise there is a peak at mid or to its left. This invariant guarantees convergence to a peak in O(log n) without requiring a fully sorted array.
